text.title: FinTech KYC BA Pack Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every FinTech KYC BA Pack application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every requirement anchors to FATF + local AML clause (cited).

rationale.source: FinTech KYC BA Pack authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Unanchored KYC requirements fail audit; FATF + local is the dual-anchor floor.

rule.statement: Customer risk classified (low / medium / high / EDD) with explicit triggers per class.

rationale.source: FinTech KYC BA Pack authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Single-tier KYC over-screens low risk and under-screens high; segmentation is regulator expectation.

rule.statement: Identity / sanctions / PEP evidence pinned with provider + retention cadence.

rationale.source: FinTech KYC BA Pack authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Evidence without provenance fails audit; cadence prevents staleness.

rule.statement: Pack names the MLRO / Money-Laundering Reporting Officer; not 'compliance team'.

rationale.source: FinTech KYC BA Pack authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: MLRO role is named in regulation; team-only ownership is non-compliant.

rule.statement: Sanctions + PEP screening cadence pinned (real-time / daily / monthly) per segment.

rationale.source: FinTech KYC BA Pack authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Lapsed screening exposes the firm to fines; cadence + segment makes it auditable.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.
